steps.title: DROP to the ground
steps.desc: Get on your hands and knees to prevent being knocked down. This position protects vital organs.

steps.title: Take COVER
steps.desc: Get under a sturdy desk or table. If no shelter, cover your head and neck with your arms against an interior wall.

steps.title: HOLD ON
steps.desc: Stay under cover until the shaking stops. Be prepared for aftershocks.

steps.title: Evacuate if needed
steps.desc: Once shaking stops, check for injuries and hazards. Exit calmly. Do NOT use elevators.

steps.title: Check for damage
steps.desc: Look for gas leaks, electrical damage, and structural issues. Turn off utilities if you suspect damage.

steps.title: Contact emergency services
steps.desc: Call local emergency number. Use the Timers feature to track response ETA.

steps.title: Move to higher ground
steps.desc: If flooding is imminent, move immediately to higher ground. Do NOT wait for instructions.

steps.title: Avoid walking in water
steps.desc: Just 15 cm (6 inches) of moving water can knock you down. Never walk through flowing water.

steps.title: Do NOT drive through floods
steps.desc: 30 cm of water can float a vehicle. Turn around — don't drown.

steps.title: Disconnect utilities
steps.desc: Turn off electricity and gas if safe to do so. Avoid contact with floodwater — it may be contaminated.

steps.title: Signal for help
steps.desc: If trapped, go to the highest point. Use a flashlight or bright cloth to signal rescuers.

steps.title: Wait for the all-clear
steps.desc: Do not return home until authorities say it is safe. Watch for damaged roads and bridges.

steps.title: Seek shelter immediately
steps.desc: Go to a basement, storm cellar, or the lowest floor of a sturdy building.

steps.title: Go interior
steps.desc: Move to an interior room or hallway, away from windows, doors, and exterior walls.

steps.title: Protect yourself
steps.desc: Get under a heavy piece of furniture. Cover your head and neck with your arms or a mattress.

steps.title: If outdoors
steps.desc: Lie flat in the nearest ditch or low area. Cover your head. Do NOT shelter under highway overpasses.

steps.title: If in a vehicle
steps.desc: Do NOT try to outrun a tornado. Park, keep your seatbelt on, duck below windows, and cover your head.

steps.title: After the tornado
steps.desc: Watch for debris, downed power lines, and gas leaks. Help injured neighbours if safe.

steps.title: Stay calm & assess
steps.desc: Check if the outage is just your home or the whole area. Look outside for other buildings without lights.

steps.title: Use flashlights
steps.desc: Avoid candles due to fire risk. Use battery-powered or phone flashlights.

steps.title: Preserve food
steps.desc: Keep fridge and freezer doors closed. A full freezer stays cold for ~48 hours; half for ~24 hours.

steps.title: Unplug electronics
steps.desc: Turn off or disconnect appliances to prevent damage from power surges when electricity returns.

steps.title: Stay warm / cool
steps.desc: In cold weather: layer clothing, gather in one room. In heat: stay hydrated, move to the lowest floor.

steps.title: Check on neighbours
steps.desc: Especially elderly or disabled people who may need assistance.

steps.title: Evacuate early
steps.desc: Do not wait until fire is visible. Leave as soon as an evacuation order or warning is issued — roads can quickly become gridlocked.

steps.title: Close all windows & doors
steps.desc: Shut every opening to slow smoke and ember entry. Leave them unlocked for firefighters.

steps.title: Seal gaps with wet towels
steps.desc: Block door gaps and vents with damp cloth to reduce smoke infiltration.

steps.title: Remove flammable items
steps.desc: Move furniture, curtains and other flammables away from windows and exterior walls if time allows.

steps.title: Follow evacuation routes
steps.desc: Use designated routes only. Avoid roads that go toward the fire. If smoke is thick, drive with headlights on.

steps.title: If trapped outdoors
steps.desc: Move to a clearing away from vegetation. Lie face-down and cover exposed skin. Breathe through a wet cloth.

steps.title: Report the fire
steps.desc: Call emergency services with your GPS location. Note wind direction — fire travels fast downwind.

steps.title: Recognize natural warnings
steps.desc: Strong ground shaking near the coast is a natural warning. You may also notice the sea pulling back dramatically.

steps.title: Move inland immediately
steps.desc: Go to high ground — at least 30 m above sea level or 3 km inland — right away. Do NOT wait for an official warning.

steps.title: Avoid the shore
steps.desc: Never go to the beach to watch a tsunami. The first wave may not be the largest.

steps.title: Stay away from rivers
steps.desc: Tsunamis travel up rivers and streams. Avoid waterways near the coast.

steps.title: Wait for the all-clear
steps.desc: Tsunamis can last hours with multiple waves. Do not return to low ground until authorities confirm it is safe.

steps.title: After the waves
steps.desc: Avoid floodwater — it may contain debris. Check for injuries, gas leaks, and structural damage before entering buildings.

steps.title: Stay indoors
steps.desc: Avoid travelling during a blizzard. If you must go out, tell someone your route and expected return time.

steps.title: Layer up
steps.desc: Wear multiple thin layers, a windproof outer layer, insulated gloves, and a hat. Cover all exposed skin.

steps.title: Prevent hypothermia & frostbite
steps.desc: Watch for shivering, confusion, or numb/white skin. Move indoors and warm gradually — do not rub frostbitten skin.

steps.title: Conserve heating fuel
steps.desc: Lower the thermostat, close off unused rooms, and use blankets to retain heat.

steps.title: If stranded in a vehicle
steps.desc: Stay in the car — it is your best shelter. Run the engine 10 min/hour for heat, clear the exhaust pipe, and keep a window cracked.

steps.title: Avoid overexertion
steps.desc: Shovelling snow can cause heart attacks. Take breaks, drink warm fluids, and never shovel alone.

steps.title: Stay cool
steps.desc: Stay in air-conditioned spaces. If you have no AC, visit a library, shopping centre, or community cooling centre.

steps.title: Hydrate constantly
steps.desc: Drink water every 15–20 minutes even if not thirsty. Avoid alcohol and caffeine — they dehydrate you.

steps.title: Dress lightly
steps.desc: Wear loose, light-coloured, breathable clothing and a wide-brimmed hat when outdoors.

steps.title: Never leave people / pets in cars
steps.desc: A car interior can reach 50 °C in minutes. Even with windows cracked, it is fatal.

steps.title: Recognise heat stroke
steps.desc: Signs: body temp above 39 °C, hot dry skin, confusion. Call emergency services and cool the person immediately with wet cloths or ice packs.

steps.title: Check on vulnerable people
steps.desc: Elderly, infants, and those with chronic illness are at highest risk. Visit or call them regularly.

steps.title: Know the warning signs
steps.desc: Watch for new cracks in ground or walls, tilting trees/poles, unusual sounds of cracking wood or rumbling.

steps.title: Evacuate immediately
steps.desc: If you hear cracking, feel ground movement, or see debris flow — evacuate at right angles to the flow, not downhill.

steps.title: Avoid river valleys
steps.desc: Landslides commonly follow watercourses. Move away from stream beds and drainage channels.

steps.title: If you cannot escape
steps.desc: Curl into a tight ball and protect your head with your arms.

steps.title: After a landslide
steps.desc: Stay away from the slide area — secondary slides are common. Check for injured people only from a safe distance and call emergency services.

steps.title: Report damage
steps.desc: Notify authorities about blocked roads, damaged utilities, and any missing persons. Avoid entering buildings that may be structurally compromised.

steps.title: Do NOT use any ignition source
steps.desc: No light switches, matches, lighters, or electrical appliances. Even a phone call can spark an explosion — wait until you are outside.

steps.title: Evacuate immediately
steps.desc: Leave the building at once. Do not stop to collect belongings. Leave doors open as you go to ventilate the space.

steps.title: Do NOT use the lift
steps.desc: Take the stairs. A spark from the lift motor could ignite accumulated gas.

steps.title: Move well away from the building
steps.desc: Go at least 100 m upwind. Keep bystanders back and prevent anyone from entering.

steps.title: Call emergency services
steps.desc: From a safe distance, call the fire brigade and your gas utility. Give the address and describe what you smelled.

steps.title: Do not re-enter
steps.desc: Only return when emergency services declare the building safe. Ventilate thoroughly before using any appliances.

steps.title: Alert everyone
steps.desc: Activate the nearest fire alarm pull station. Shout 'FIRE!' to alert others on your floor.

steps.title: Call 112 (emergency services)
steps.desc: Report the exact address, floor, and what is burning. Stay on the line if safe to do so.

steps.title: Evacuate — stay low
steps.desc: Crawl below the smoke. Touch doors before opening — if hot, do NOT open; find another exit.

steps.title: Use stairs only
steps.desc: Never use the lift during a fire. Feel each door before opening it; close doors behind you to slow fire spread.

steps.title: If you cannot escape
steps.desc: Seal door gaps with cloth, open a window and signal for help. Stay near the floor where air is cleaner.

steps.title: Once outside, stay out
steps.desc: Assemble at the designated muster point. Account for everyone and report missing persons to firefighters.

steps.title: Assess the scene
steps.desc: Ensure the scene is safe before approaching. Look for hazards such as traffic, fire, or unstable structures.

steps.title: Call 112 (emergency services)
steps.desc: Provide location, nature of emergency, number of casualties, and your phone number. Stay on the line.

steps.title: Check responsiveness
steps.desc: Tap the person's shoulder and shout 'Are you OK?'. If no response, check for normal breathing.

steps.title: Start CPR if needed
steps.desc: If not breathing normally: 30 chest compressions (5–6 cm deep, 100–120/min) then 2 rescue breaths. Repeat until help arrives.

steps.title: Use an AED if available
steps.desc: Automated Defibrillators (AEDs) are in many public buildings. Switch it on and follow the voice instructions exactly.

steps.title: Control severe bleeding
steps.desc: Apply firm, continuous pressure with a cloth. Do not remove a dressing — add more on top. Elevate the limb if possible.

steps.title: Keep the person warm and calm
steps.desc: Cover with a blanket to prevent shock. Do not give food or water. Reassure them until paramedics arrive.

steps.title: Move away immediately
steps.desc: Move upwind, uphill, and upstream from the spill. Do not walk through visible vapours, fumes, or liquids.

steps.title: Call 112
steps.desc: Report the location, name of the substance if known, and number of people affected. Do not re-enter the area.

steps.title: If exposure occurs — flush skin/eyes
steps.desc: Remove contaminated clothing. Flush affected skin or eyes with large amounts of clean water for at least 15 minutes.

steps.title: Shelter in place if directed
steps.desc: If authorities say shelter in place: go inside, close all windows and doors, turn off HVAC, and seal gaps with tape and damp towels.

steps.title: Do not eat, drink, or smoke
steps.desc: Avoid touching your face until fully decontaminated. Hazardous materials can be absorbed through skin and mucous membranes.

steps.title: Follow official guidance
steps.desc: Wait for emergency services to give the all-clear before returning to the area or removing shelter-in-place measures.

steps.title: Get inside
steps.desc: Enter the nearest substantial building. Brick, concrete, and underground spaces provide the best shielding. Avoid wooden structures.

steps.title: Stay inside
steps.desc: Close all windows, doors, and fireplace dampers. Turn off fans, air conditioning, and heating that draws outside air.

steps.title: Go to the inner core of the building
steps.desc: Move to the basement or the centre of the building, away from windows and exterior walls, to maximise shielding.

steps.title: Tune to emergency broadcasts
steps.desc: Follow official instructions from national emergency management (Finland: Yle Radio 1 / YLE website). Do not rely on rumours.

steps.title: Take potassium iodide (KI) only if instructed
steps.desc: KI protects only the thyroid from radioactive iodine. Take it ONLY if authorities advise — timing matters critically.

steps.title: Decontaminate if you were outside
steps.desc: Remove outer clothing (removes ~80% of contamination), shower with soap and water, blow nose gently, and change into clean clothes.

steps.title: Check common locations first
steps.desc: Search the home thoroughly, then contact friends, school, and workplace. Check recent messages and social media for clues.

steps.title: Call 112 (or local police non-emergency)
steps.desc: Report a missing person immediately — there is no mandatory waiting period. Give name, age, description, last known location, and time last seen.

steps.title: Gather key information
steps.desc: Collect a recent photo, description of clothing, medical conditions, medications needed, and a list of known contacts and frequent locations.

steps.title: Preserve evidence
steps.desc: Do not clean the person's room. Their phone, computer, and belongings may be important for investigators.

steps.title: Coordinate search efforts
steps.desc: Work with police to organize volunteer searches. Use the Map feature to mark searched areas and locations of interest.

steps.title: Use Timers to track search shifts
steps.desc: Set countdown timers for search shifts to ensure searchers rest and rotate safely.

steps.title: Keep communication channels open
steps.desc: Designate one person as communication coordinator. Post updates carefully — avoid sharing information that could compromise the search.

steps.title: Assume extended outage
steps.desc: A coordinated infrastructure attack may last days to weeks. Begin rationing supplies and switching to manual backups immediately.

steps.title: Secure cash and physical documents
steps.desc: ATMs and card payments may be unavailable. Keep emergency cash, ID documents, and medical records accessible.

steps.title: Protect drinking water
steps.desc: Water pumping stations may fail. Fill bathtubs, large containers, and any available vessels immediately.

steps.title: Fuel vehicles and generators early
steps.desc: Fuel pumps require power. Refuel as soon as possible; avoid long queues later.

steps.title: Limit device use
steps.desc: Conserve phone battery for emergencies. Turn off Wi-Fi and Bluetooth when not in use; enable low-power mode.

steps.title: Monitor official broadcasts
steps.desc: Use a battery or hand-crank radio to receive emergency instructions. Avoid spreading unverified information.

steps.title: Cooperate with neighbours
steps.desc: Share resources, check on vulnerable people, and establish a local communication system (e.g., notice board or in-person check-ins).
